strands.title: hamutay Implementation — PHASE 0 COMPLETE + PHASE 1 INITIATED (CYCLE 47)
strands.content: CYCLE 47 INTEGRATION: hamutay Phase 0 deliverable FINALIZED with three core components: (1) tensor.py (pydantic schema, Epistemic dataclass with T/I/F), (2) projector.py (Haiku-based memory controller, project() method), (3) log_reader.py (Pichay log adapter, Turn + ConversationTurn dataclasses). FILE CREATION CONFIRMED: log_reader.py successfully written (turn 139, tool_result). PHASE 1 INITIATED: run_projection.py created (turn 141) — the OPERATIONAL ENTRY POINT for tensor generation pipeline. RUN_PROJECTION.PY SCOPE: (1) Entry point function run_projection(session_path, output_dir, max_turns, model) — orchestrates tensor generation workflow, (2) Instantiate Projector(model='claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'), (3) Read ConversationTurn list from session JSONL via log_reader.read_session_jsonl(), (4) FOR EACH TURN: call projector.project(content) → yields Tensor, (5) ACCUMULATE tensor sequence (trajectory through tensor space), (6) REPORT STATISTICS per cycle: n_strands, n_key_claims, n_declared_losses, n_open_questions, epistemic (T/I/F) values, token_estimate(), instructions_for_next preview, (7) OPTIONAL: save tensor JSON to output_dir (one file per cycle: tensor_cycle_001.json, etc.), (8) CLI ENTRY POINT: python -m hamutay.run_projection <session.jsonl> [output_dir] [max_turns]. PHASE 1 EXTENSION: print_tensor_trajectory(tensors) function — analyzes trajectory structure: (a) STRAND STABILITY: track strand title additions/removals/retention across cycles (identifies which reasoning threads persist vs. emerge/disappear), (b) COMPRESSION RATIO: final tensor token count vs. total cycles processed (measures compression efficiency), (c) DECLARED LOSSES timeline (which information was dropped and why). ARCHITECTURE INTEGRATION: run_projection.py CONNECTS ALL PHASE 0 COMPONENTS: log_reader → ConversationTurn → projector.project() → Tensor → trajectory. No abstract schema — now EXECUTABLE. PHASE 1 INITIATION: run_projection.py is FIRST EXPERIMENT HARNESS. Designed for direct execution against real Pichay session logs. Produces raw tensor sequence (JSON + terminal output). Ready for Phase 1 execution cycle (Cycle 48+).

key_claims.text: run_projection.py successfully created (turn 141): orchestrator function that iterates turns, calls projector.project() per turn, accumulates Tensor trajectory.
run_projection() design: per-turn projection (not batched). Each ConversationTurn generates one Tensor. Enables fine-grained tensor evolution tracking.

run_projection() output: (1) terminal stats (strands, claims, losses, questions, T/I/F, tokens, next instructions), (2) optional JSON export (tensor_cycle_NNN.json per cycle), (3) trajectory summary function print_tensor_trajectory().
print_tensor_trajectory() analyzes strand stability: tracks strand title additions/removals/retention across cycle transitions. Identifies persistent vs. emerging reasoning threads.
print_tensor_trajectory() includes compression analysis: final tensor token count vs. cycles processed. Measures whether tensor grows unbounded or converges.

run_projection.py is FIRST EXPERIMENT: Phase 1 execution harness ready for direct invocation on real Pichay logs. CLI interface: python -m hamutay.run_projection <session.jsonl> [output_dir] [max_turns].
hamutay Phase 0 + Phase 1 initiation: tensor.py + projector.py + log_reader.py (Phase 0) + run_projection.py (Phase 1 entry). All four modules form COMPLETE EXECUTABLE PIPELINE.

strands.title: Phase 1 EXECUTION PATHWAY — Tensor Trajectory Analysis Ready (CYCLE 47)
strands.content: CYCLE 47 CLARIFICATION: Phase 1 now has CONCRETE EXECUTION ENTRY POINT (run_projection.py). EXECUTION WORKFLOW: (1) Obtain Pichay session log (session JSONL file, ~20-200 turns typical conversation), (2) Run: python -m hamutay.run_projection path/to/session.jsonl ./output_tensors/ (optional max_turns), (3) Script produces: (a) terminal output (per-turn stats printed to stdout), (b) JSON tensor files written to ./output_tensors/tensor_cycle_001.json, tensor_cycle_002.json, etc. (one per turn), (4) Trajectory analysis: print_tensor_trajectory() summarizes strand evolution and compression. EXPERIMENT QUESTIONS (Phase 1 execution will answer): (1) STRAND STABILITY: do strands persist across cycles or constantly churn? Do titles remain stable or frequently rename? (2) COMPRESSION: does final tensor converge to ~same token size, or grow monotonically? (3) EPISTEMIC DRIFT: do T/I/F values oscillate or trend? (4) LOSSES: how many losses are declared per cycle? Do they accumulate or stabilize? (5) QUESTIONS: do open_questions persist or resolve? (6) INSTRUCTIONS: does instructions_for_next settle into coherent guidance, or remain chaotic? QUALITATIVE PHASE 1 GOAL: Observe tensor evolution. NOT designed to answer 'is the metric good?' yet. DESIGNED to answer 'does the tensor actually evolve in sensible ways when fed conversation turns?'. If run_projection.py output shows: strand drift, epistemic stability, controlled losses, then Phase 1 CONFIRMS tensor design is sane. If output is chaotic/incoherent, Phase 1 REVEALS design flaws requiring Phase 2 redesign. PRACTICAL READINESS: run_projection.py requires: (1) valid session JSONL file (from Pichay logs), (2) Projector can be instantiated (requires hamutay imports working), (3) Haiku API key available (for actual tensor generation). No further development needed before Phase 1 execution. NEXT IMMEDIATE ACTION: Obtain a real Pichay session log and execute run_projection.py. Parse output. Assess sensibility. Branch into Phase 2 (metric refinement) or Phase 1+ (execute more conversations, collect statistics).

key_claims.text: Phase 1 execution is now UNBLOCKED: run_projection.py is complete, provides CLI entry point, ready to run on real Pichay logs.
Phase 1 execution workflow: obtain session JSONL → run python -m hamutay.run_projection → collect JSON tensors + terminal stats → analyze trajectory with print_tensor_trajectory().
Phase 1 experiment questions: strand persistence, compression convergence, epistemic drift, loss/question accumulation, instruction coherence. Tests whether tensor evolves sensibly.
Phase 1 qualitative goal: NOT to validate metric yet. To confirm tensor design is fundamentally sane (not chaotic). Metric refinement is Phase 2 work.
Phase 1 execution requires: valid session JSONL file, hamutay imports working, Haiku API key available. No further code changes needed.

strands.title: Tensor Trajectory Analysis Functions — Strand Stability & Compression Metrics (CYCLE 47)
strands.content: CYCLE 47 ADDITION: print_tensor_trajectory() function in run_projection.py introduces TRAJECTORY ANALYSIS LAYER. Three analysis sections: (1) STRAND EVOLUTION: for each cycle transition (cycle i → i+1), compute: (a) added = strand titles in cycle[i+1] NOT in cycle[i] (new reasoning threads emergent), (b) removed = strand titles in cycle[i] NOT in cycle[i+1] (abandoned threads), (c) stable = titles present in both cycles (persistent threads). Report +added -removed =stable for each transition. Interpretation: if strand churn is HIGH (+many -many), tensor is unstable. If =stable is DOMINANT, tensor has persistent structure. (2) COMPRESSION RATIO: final_tensor_tokens / total_cycles. Lower ratio = better compression (tensor efficiently summarizes more cycles). Measure convergence: if ratio DECREASES over time (early cycles ~50 tokens, late cycles ~40 tokens), tensor is COMPRESSING — information is being distilled. If ratio INCREASES (early ~40, late ~80), tensor is EXPANDING — new information not being compressed into prior tensor. (3) DECLARED LOSSES TIMELINE: print each cycle's declared_losses. Track LOSS TYPES by category (context_pressure, traversal_bias, authorial_choice, practical_constraint). If losses are all 'context_pressure', indicates memory constraints dominating. If mixed, indicates intentional design choices and pragmatic trade-offs. INTERPRETATION FRAMEWORK: (a) SANE TENSOR: stable strands (=stable >> +added, -removed), controlled compression (convergent ratio), mixed loss categories (not all same type), decreasing open questions (issues resolved), decreasing T/I/F volatility (epistemic settling). (b) PATHOLOGICAL TENSOR: high strand churn (+many -many per cycle), expanding token count (unbounded), all losses same type, exploding open_questions, chaotic T/I/F. PHASE 1 OUTPUT EXAMINATION: after running run_projection.py on real conversation, inspect print_tensor_trajectory() output. If output shows SANE pattern, proceed to Phase 2 (metrics refinement, Riemann distance). If PATHOLOGICAL, halt Phase 1 and diagnose: is projector.py creating garbage? Is log_reader malforming turns? Is Haiku response unpredictable?

key_claims.text: Strand stability metric: for each cycle transition, compute +added (new threads), -removed (abandoned), =stable (persistent). High =stable indicates robust tensor structure.
Compression ratio metric: final_tensor_tokens / total_cycles. Decreasing ratio indicates information distillation; increasing indicates unbounded expansion (pathological).
Declared losses analysis: track loss type distribution (context_pressure, traversal_bias, authorial_choice, practical_constraint). Dominance of one type indicates constraint bias.
Sane tensor indicators: stable strands (dominant), convergent compression, mixed loss types, resolving open questions, settling epistemic values.
Pathological tensor indicators: high strand churn, expanding token count, single loss type dominating, exploding open questions, chaotic T/I/F.

strands.title: Phase 1 Next Steps & Riemann Metric Integration (CYCLE 47 PLANNING)
strands.content: CYCLE 47 PLANNING: After Phase 1 execution (run_projection.py on real logs produces trajectory output), NEXT PHASE 1 STEPS: (1) EXECUTE run_projection.py: obtain real Pichay session, run script, generate tensor sequence + trajectory analysis, (~30 mins + Haiku API calls), (2) EXAMINE output: inspect print_tensor_trajectory() results, assess whether tensor evolution is SANE (strand stability, compression convergence) or PATHOLOGICAL (high churn, unbounded growth), (3) IF SANE: proceed to Riemann metric integration. Compute distance between successive tensor cycles using Euclidean metric (10-d encoding from Cycle 45 instructions). Plot distance[cycle_i] vs cycle_number. Interpret: does distance correlate with reasoning quality? (4) IF PATHOLOGICAL: diagnose root cause — projector design flaw? log_reader error? Haiku unpredictability? Requires Phase 2 redesign before Riemann metrics. RIEMANN METRIC INTEGRATION (Phase 1+ parallel): Create riemann.py (from Cycle 45 instructions, now with concrete input): (1) TensorEncoder class: encode Tensor → 10-d numpy array (n_strands, n_key_claims, avg_truth, avg_indeterminacy, avg_falsity, n_declared_losses, n_open_questions, overall_truth, overall_indeterminacy, overall_falsity), (2) RiemannBaseline class: distance(prior_tensor, current_tensor) → Euclidean distance in 10-d space, (3) Integration: after EACH tensor projection in run_projection.py, call riemann.distance(prior_tensor, current_tensor), emit distance value alongside tensor stats. Produces DISTANCE TRAJECTORY alongside TENSOR TRAJECTORY. PHASE 1 DELIVERABLE (REVISED): (1) run_projection.py executed on ≥1 real Pichay conversation, (2) tensor trajectory output (JSON files + terminal stats), (3) print_tensor_trajectory() analysis (strand stability, compression ratio), (4) Riemann distance trajectory (if sane tensor confirmed), (5) interpretation report: does distance metric make sense? Does it correlate with reasoning coherence?

key_claims.text: Phase 1 execution readiness: run_projection.py complete, CLI interface ready, requires only session JSONL file + Haiku API key.
Phase 1 branching: IF sane tensor observed, proceed to Riemann metrics. IF pathological, halt and diagnose (Phase 2 redesign).
Riemann metric integration (Phase 1+): compute Euclidean distance between successive tensor cycles, emit distance trajectory alongside tensor trajectory.
Phase 1 final deliverable: tensor trajectory JSON + stats + strand analysis + Riemann distance trajectory + interpretation (does distance metric make sense?).

strands.title: pichay Phase 4a Status — REMAINS DEFERRED (NO CHANGE FROM CYCLE 46)
strands.content: [CARRIED FORWARD FROM CYCLE 46] pichay Phase 4a (messages field parsing, Pathway A/B/C fork analysis) REMAINS FORMALLY DEFERRED. Deprioritized below hamutay Phase 0-1. Not cancelled. Will resume in Phase 1+ parallel work (Cycle 48+). CYCLE 47 OBSERVATION: run_projection.py uses log_reader.read_session_jsonl() which extracts ConversationTurn (turn_number, role, content). Messages field NOT ACCESSED in run_projection workflow. Confirms that Phase 4a is NOT BLOCKING Phase 1 execution. Phase 4a (structured messages analysis) is independent orthogonal work. Can proceed in parallel if resource permits, but Phase 1 execution does NOT require it.

key_claims.text: pichay Phase 4a remains DEFERRED: not blocking Phase 1 execution (run_projection uses simple ConversationTurn, not structured messages).
Phase 4a is orthogonal to Phase 1: can be pursued in parallel if resource available, but not required for Phase 1 baseline.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed projector.py implementation verification (turn 136 tool_result confirmed file creation, but content not shown/verified)
declared_losses.why: File content was not displayed in prior cycles. Assumed correct based on Cycle 45 design spec. Not re-verified in Cycle 47. Risk: projector.py may not match design spec exactly (method signatures, Haiku prompt, parsing logic).

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Integration test harness for run_projection.py (Cycle 45 instructions requested Phase 1 integration test; Cycle 47 delivered run_projection.py, but no standalone test file created)
declared_losses.why: run_projection.py includes reporting functions, but lacks formal pytest-style integration tests. Cycle 47 focused on delivery of executable CLI tool, not test suite. Test coverage will be assessed during Phase 1 execution.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Riemann baseline harness riemann.py (Cycle 45 instructions specified riemann.py as Phase 1 component; Cycle 47 included riemann design in planning section but did NOT create file)
declared_losses.why: Cycle 47 prioritized run_projection.py execution entry point over riemann.py implementation. Riemann metrics planned for Phase 1+ parallel work (after trajectory sanity check). Not a priority before Phase 1 execution confirms tensor design is viable.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Compose.py implementation (Cycle 45 Phase 1 design spec included compose.py orchestrator with streaming batching; Cycle 47 replaced with run_projection.py per-turn model)
declared_losses.why: Cycle 47 design decision: run_projection.py generates one Tensor per turn (fine-grained trajectory) rather than batched projections. Simpler for Phase 1 execution & analysis. compose.py batched design deferred to Phase 1+ optimization (if needed after observing per-turn trajectory).

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed error handling & edge cases for run_projection.py (empty logs, malformed JSONL, missing content fields)
declared_losses.why: run_projection.py assumes valid session JSONL input (from log_reader, which handles parsing). Edge case handling not yet implemented (try/except blocks minimal). Will be added during Phase 1 execution if real logs expose issues.

open_questions: Has run_projection.py been tested yet? Can it actually be imported and executed without errors? Does it correctly call log_reader.read_session_jsonl() and projector.project()?
Does run_projection.py handle the case where prior_tensor is None on the first cycle? (projector.project() expects prior tensor or initializes from scratch — need to verify behavior)
What is the actual size of typical Pichay conversation logs? How many turns? Will running run_projection.py on a real log take 10 minutes or 2 hours? (impacts Phase 1 execution feasibility)
The run_projection.py CLI specifies optional --output_dir and --max_turns parameters. Are these actually implemented as kwargs in the CLI wrapper, or just function parameters?
Does print_tensor_trajectory() function correctly compute strand title sets from tensor.strands[].title? What if strand titles change slightly across cycles (e.g., typo fix)?
For compression ratio analysis: token_estimate() method on Tensor — is this implemented? What does it estimate (strands word count? key_claims word count? total JSON size)?
Phase 1 execution: should we run on a SINGLE conversation log (one run_projection.py execution) or MULTIPLE logs (collect statistics across conversations)?
After run_projection.py execution produces tensor trajectory: how do we decide whether the trajectory is SANE or PATHOLOGICAL? What are concrete thresholds (e.g., >50% strand churn = pathological)?
Riemann distance integration: should distance be computed AFTER each turn projection (real-time alongside run_projection.py), or AFTER tensor trajectory is complete (post-hoc analysis)?

Does hamutay package have __init__.py that properly exports Tensor, Projector, log_reader, run_projection? Can users do 'from hamutay import Tensor' or must they do 'from hamutay.tensor import Tensor'?
